_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize 5 FL state legislators (Davis SD-5, Griffitts HD-6, Bradley SD-6, Shoaf HD-7, Leek SD-7). Currency fixes: Davis & Leek not up until 2028; Griffitts not running 2026. Cross-party catch: Bradley NAY on HB1557.

tracie-davis@FL.notes_set: Democrat, FL Senate District 5 (Duval/Jacksonville). CURRENCY FIX: NOT up in 2026 — won reelection Nov 2024, term to Nov 2028; elected Senate Democratic Caucus leader for 2026-2028. Consistent progressive across all 10 categories. HARD: voted NO on SB300 (6-week ban, 2023); prime sponsor of SB870 (2025) to REPEAL the 6-week ban and S1308 'Reproductive Freedom'; opposed SB254 (trans-care ban for minors), called it 'just healthcare'; opposed HB1 (2023 universal vouchers); sponsored S1492 'Instruction on Reproductive Health'; Senate sponsor of a state Voting Rights Act (SB1582) w/ same-day registration; sponsored gun-control bill S1336; backs universal background checks + red-flag laws (2022 voter guide). ONE credit (cross-issue): sponsored 'Donna's Law' eliminating the statute of limitations for sexual battery against minors — a victim-justice measure. Religion: no evidence found.

0.note: Voted NO on SB300 6-week ban; prime sponsor of SB870 to repeal it and of S1308 'Reproductive Freedom' — affirmatively pro-abortion-rights.

1.note: Sponsors repeal of abortion limits; opposite of abortion abolition.

4.note: No evidence of PP/NARAL/EMILY's List funding either way.

0.note: No documented same-sex-marriage position.

1.note: No documented vote on civil unions/domestic partnerships.

2.note: Opposed SB254 trans-care ban; characterized gender-affirming care as 'just healthcare' — contrary to affirming immutable biological sex.

4.note: Opposed SB254 (restricting trans procedures/drag/bathroom for minors); record favors LGBTQ-identity policy, contrary to opposing its promotion.

0.note: Opposed HB1 universal vouchers; argued it chips away at public-school funding.

1.note: No documented parental-consent vote.

2.note: Sponsored S1492 'Instruction on Reproductive Health' — supports comprehensive sex-ed, contrary to opposing it.

No evidence on hand-counted paper ballots.

2.note: Supports early-voting expansion and same-day registration (SB1582) — contrary to single-day in-person voting.

3.note: Opposes barriers to mail-in ballots, supports expansion — contrary to opposing mass mail-in.

4.note: No evidence on private election funding.

0.note: No affirmative pro-police advocacy documented.

1.note: No evidence on bail/rogue prosecutors.

2.note: Sponsored 'Donna's Law' eliminating the statute of limitations for sexual battery against minors — proportional punishment of evildoers / victim justice (cross-issue credit).

griff-griffitts-jr@FL.notes_set: Republican, FL House District 6 (Bay County/Panama City); former Bay County Commissioner. CURRENCY FIX: NOT running in 2026 — announced 2026-05-11 he will not seek reelection ('time to step back and focus on family, business'); HD-6 is now an open seat. Took office Nov 2022, so HB5 (2022) and HB1557 (2022) predate him (not applicable). Consistent conservative, no cross-party deviation. HARD: voted YEA on SB300 (6-week ban); cosponsored HB543 (constitutional carry); prime sponsor of medical-conscience/anti-mandate bills HB1013/HB1015 and cosponsor HB1403 'Medical Conscience'. Party-line marquee: SB254 (trans-care ban, passed along party lines). Near-unanimous SB7054 anti-CBDC. Campaign site: supports voter ID, opposes federal voting/curriculum mandates.

0.note: Voted YEA on SB300, the 6-week Heartbeat Protection Act — affirms sanctity of unborn life.

1.note: 6-week ban is a restriction, not full abolition; no abolition vote.

0.note: No direct statement on marriage definition.

1.note: No evidence on same-sex marriage/civil unions.

2.note: In GOP majority on SB254 (gender-care ban for minors), passed House along party lines.

4.note: No direct vote on LGBTQ-in-schools (HB1557 predates his term).

0.note: In GOP majority on HB1 (2023) universal school-choice vouchers.

1.note: No evidence on parental medical consent.

1.note: Prime sponsor of HB1013/HB1015 (anti-discrimination on health-care choices) and cosponsor of HB1403 'Medical Conscience' — conscience exemptions for medical providers.

jennifer-bradley@FL.notes_set: Republican, FL Senate District 6 (Clay/NE Florida); wife of former Sen. Rob Bradley. Running for reelection (R primary Aug 18 2026). Solidly conservative voting record with ONE documented cross-party LIBERAL deviation. HARD roll calls (flsenate.gov): YEA on SB300 (6-wk ban) and HB5 (15-wk ban); YEA on SB254 (trans-care ban); YEA on HB1 (universal vouchers); YEA on HB931 (school chaplains); YEA on SB7054 (anti-CBDC); YEA on SB1718 (E-Verify/immigration); YEA on HB543 (constitutional carry); NRA-PVF endorsed 2020. DEVIATION (HARD): voted NAY on HB1557 (2022 'Parental Rights in Education'/'Don't Say Gay') — one of only two Senate Republicans against; her stated reason was the bill's private cause of action (litigation exposure for school districts), not endorsement of the curriculum. Freedom Index 71% (moderate-conservative). Note: iVoterGuide 'life' quotes were a template — she answered none of their questions; disregarded.

0.note: Voted YEA on both SB300 (6-week) and HB5 (15-week) abortion bans.

1.note: Restrictions, not abolition; no abolition vote.

0.note: No direct statement on marriage definition.

1.note: No evidence on same-sex marriage/civil unions.

2.note: Voted YEA on SB254, ban on gender procedures for minors.

4.note: Mixed: YEA on SB254 but NAY on HB1557 (scored under family q2); no clean LGBTQ-policy signal here.

0.note: Voted YEA on HB1 (2023) universal school-choice vouchers.

1.note: No evidence on parental medical consent.

2.note: CROSS-PARTY: voted NAY on HB1557 restricting K-3 SOGI classroom instruction (one of 2 Senate R's against). Stated reason was the litigation/private-cause-of-action provision, not support for the curriculum.

3.note: No confirmed vote on porn age-verification.

0.note: No direct evidence.

1.note: No evidence on conscience exemptions.

3.note: Voted YEA on HB931 (2024) volunteer school chaplains — religious presence in public institutions.

0.note: Voted YEA on SB7054 removing CBDC from the definition of money.

jason-shoaf@FL.notes_set: Republican, FL House District 7 (Big Bend/Panhandle, Port St. Joe); first elected 2019 special election. Running for reelection (R primary Aug 18 2026). Thorough, consistent conservative — no cross-party deviation. Self-described 'unabashed Christian.' NRA Lifetime member; Florida Right to Life endorsed (2024). HARD roll calls (Digital Democracy Project / flsenate.gov): YEA + cosponsor on HB5 (15-wk ban); YEA on SB300 (6-wk ban) and HB1517 (wrongful death of unborn); YEA on HB1639 (Biological Sex), HB1421 (trans-care), SB1674 (bathroom); YEA on HB1 vouchers + HB443 charters; YEA on HB3/HB1539 (materials harmful to minors, age-verification) and 2024 social-media-minors bill; YEA on HB931 (chaplains), HB1009 (religious expression K-12), HB1403 (medical conscience); YEA on SB7054 (anti-CBDC) and HB3 (2023 anti-ESG/'corporate activism'); YEA on SB7050 (elections omnibus); YEA on SB450 (death penalty) + pro-police bills; YEA on SB1718 (E-Verify), HB5B (migrant relocation), SB2B (immigration enforcement); YEA on HB543 carry + HB17/HB1615/HB1543/SB214 (gun-restriction rollbacks). Correction to prior note: he VOTED for HB543 but was NOT a cosponsor.

0.note: YEA + cosponsor on HB5 (15-wk); YEA on SB300 (6-wk) and HB1517 (wrongful death of unborn); '100% pro-life'.

1.note: Restrictions, not abolition; no abolition vote.

3.note: SB1098 was procedural/probate, not euthanasia advocacy; no clear evidence.

4.note: Florida Right to Life endorsed (HARD pro-life); stated 'not one cent of taxpayer funds to Planned Parenthood'; no evidence of abortion-industry funding.

4.note: YEA on HB1639 and SB1674 — opposes LGBTQ-ideology in public policy.

0.note: YEA on HB1 (2023 vouchers), HB3C, HB443 (charters) — universal school choice.

tom-leek@FL.notes_set: Republican, FL Senate District 7 (St. Johns/Putnam/Flagler/Volusia); FL House 2016-2024, elected to Senate 2024. CURRENCY FIX: NOT up in 2026 — won the seat Nov 2024, term to Nov 2028. Consistent conservative, no cross-party deviation; this refinement also clears the prior record's uncited TRUEs and a bogus FALSE on LGBTQ-in-schools. HARD (Freedom Index roll calls / flsenate.gov): YEA + cosponsor on HB5 (15-wk ban); YEA on SB300 (6-wk ban) and SB404 (2020 parental consent for minor's abortion); COSPONSOR of HB1557 (2022 'Parental Rights in Education'); YEA on HB543 (constitutional carry); NO on SB7026 (2018 post-Parkland gun-control package — pro-gun direction); YEA on HB1543/HB1223 (lower purchase age) and SB214 (financial privacy); YEA on HB1205 (initiative-petition restriction); YEA on SB1804 (2025 death penalty for child sex traffickers); sponsored/enacted 'Criminal Offenses Against Law Enforcement' and 'Felony Battery' as Senator. Florida Right to Life endorsed (2024 primary + general); NRA 'A' + endorsement (2024). Freedom Index 78%.

0.note: YEA + cosponsor on HB5 (15-wk); YEA on SB300 (6-wk) and SB404 (parental consent); Florida Right to Life endorsed.

1.note: Restrictions, not abolition; no abolition vote.

4.note: Florida Right to Life endorsed (HARD pro-life); no evidence of abortion-industry funding.
